title: World of Claudecraft — AI agent: final changeset
summary: Everything needed to (A) make the game cleanly agent-drivable upstream, and (B) have an agent actually play the live client with no game change. Part B includes the non-obvious integration fixes discovered while getting Claude to play.

partA_upstreamChanges.purpose: Make src/sim multi-agent + LLM-readable. Additive, pure, backward-compatible. Needed for the HEADLESS / multi-agent / RL path; NOT required for in-browser take-over (Part B).
partA_upstreamChanges.commit: feat(sim): multi-agent observations + observe() for LLM/RL agents

changes.action: add per-pid public read accessors after the primary getters (mirror them, resolve by pid, fall back to primary)
code: playerOf(pid?: number): Entity { return this.resolve(pid)?.e ?? this.player; }
moveInputOf(pid?: number): MoveInput { return this.resolve(pid)?.meta.moveInput ?? this.moveInput; }
inventoryOf(pid?: number): InvSlot[] { return this.resolve(pid)?.meta.inventory ?? this.inventory; }
xpOf(pid?: number): number { return this.resolve(pid)?.meta.xp ?? this.xp; }
knownOf(pid?: number): ResolvedAbility[] { return this.resolve(pid)?.meta.known ?? this.known; }

changes.action: thread optional pid through applyAction(sim,action,pid?) and encodeObs(sim,pid?); replace sim.player/moveInput/inventory/known/xp with the *Of(pid) accessors; pass pid to targetNearestEnemy/startAutoAttack/interact/stopAutoAttack/castAbilityBySlot/useItem/releaseSpirit (all already accept pid).
add observe(sim, pid?) returning a structured JSON snapshot (self/target/nearby-mobs as dist+bearingDeg/ready-abilities) — the LLM-readable twin of encodeObs. Uses existing angleTo/normAngle/dist2d.
add vitest: encodeObs length == obsSize(); applyAction maps; multi-agent pid independence; observe() shape. Keep tests/architecture.test.ts (sim purity) green.

partB_inBrowserTakeover.purpose: Let an agent play the LIVE client (load the game yourself, then it takes over). NO game change required.
partB_inBrowserTakeover.entryPoint: After load, src/main.ts exposes window.__game = { sim, world, input, hud, controller, ... }. Read window.__game.sim for state; call the same public Sim methods the HUD calls to act.
partB_inBrowserTakeover.perceive: Read window.__game.sim: player = sim.player; entities = sim.entities (Map); quest log = sim.players.get(sim.primaryId).questLog (NOT sim.player.questLog).

act.move: Real key events (W/A/D) — the input layer rebuilds moveInput from the keyboard each frame, so directly setting sim.moveInput is clobbered.
act.facing: sim.player.facing = Math.atan2(target.x - p.x, target.z - p.z)  // the game's facingToward convention (src/game/click_move.ts). Then hold W to run straight at the target.
act.combat: sim.targetEntity(id); sim.startAutoAttack(); sim.castAbility('fireball')  // melee range 5, Fireball range 30.
act.quests: sim.acceptQuest('q_wolves') at the giver; sim.turnInQuest('q_wolves') when sim.questState('q_wolves')==='ready'.

integrationGotchas.problem: Headless Chromium throttles requestAnimationFrame/timers for backgrounded pages, which FREEZES the game's tick loop (world doesn't advance; only ~17 frames in 48s).
Setting sim.moveInput.forward=true in JS does nothing — the input system overwrites moveInput from real keyboard state every frame.
integrationGotchas.fix: Drive movement with real key events (page.keyboard.down('w')), not by mutating moveInput.

integrationGotchas.problem: Character would wander, never reaching the mob (turn-key math didn't converge).
integrationGotchas.fix: Set facing directly to Math.atan2(dx,dz) and hold W — matches the game's own click-to-move, no turn-key guessing.

integrationGotchas.problem: Offline character creation: clicking the class button via DOM .click()/text match silently failed — character always spawned as the default Warrior.
integrationGotchas.fix: There are TWO [data-class="X"] elements (one hidden); click the VISIBLE one: locator('[data-class="mage"]:visible'). Verify sim.player.resourceType after spawn.

integrationGotchas.problem: Quest never looked accepted (the agent kept re-accepting → the in-game 'beep' error).
integrationGotchas.fix: Read the quest log from the player META (sim.players.get(sim.primaryId).questLog), not the entity. And call acceptQuest ONCE; re-accepting a held quest emits an error.

integrationGotchas.problem: talkToNpc(npcId) alone did not accept q_wolves.
integrationGotchas.fix: Call sim.acceptQuest('q_wolves') explicitly while standing at the giver (within INTERACT_RANGE).

partB_inBrowserTakeover.scope: Offline/practice mode only. The live multiplayer server is authoritative and runs anti-bot (src/sim/client_challenge.ts, src/net/native_attestation.ts); driving it externally would desync and/or be flagged. Coordinate with maintainers for any online/server-side agent.

partC_clientArtifacts.note: In claudehead/worldofclaudecraft-quests (this repo) — no game change.

files.agent/woc-agent.user.js: Tampermonkey userscript: F8 take-over of window.__game.sim; two-tier loop (per-frame executor + ~1Hz brain); local Gemma via Ollama with a scripted fallback.
files.agent/skills/<class>.json: Per-class playbooks (abilities/rotation/rules) generated from CLASSES/ABILITIES by tools/generate-agent-skill.ts.
files.tools/generate-agent-skill.ts: Generator for the skill packs (wired into tools/build.sh).
files.harness (demonstrated): Playwright control harness that opens the game, exposes obs via C:/tmp/woc-obs.json, and applies actions from C:/tmp/woc-ctl.json — used for Claude live-play + full-screen recording (ffmpeg gdigrab).

decisionLoop.twoTier: Brain (LLM: Claude or local Gemma) decides a high-level intent ~1x/sec from observe(); a deterministic executor enacts it every frame (face+approach+cast/attack/loot). Keeps it responsive and cheap.
decisionLoop.knowledgeSource: The agent plays informed by this repo's generated guide data (quests, bestiary stats, per-class rotations) — e.g. q_wolves = kill 8 Forest Wolves (lvl 1-2), Mage rotation Fireball>Frostbolt>Fire Blast.
decisionLoop.verifiedRun: Mage: walk to Marshal Redbrook -> accept 'Wolves at the Door' -> Fireball 8 Forest Wolves -> turn in -> reward + level 2 -> follow-up quests (q_greyjaw, q_bandits) unlocked.
